get.description: Get orders that are served and ready for billing
Get all orders with optional filters

post.description: Create new order

delete.description: Delete order ΓÇö hard-deletes from the database so it never reappears

patch.description:
Update order status with automatic flow integration.

Flow:
- placed ΓåÆ preparing: Triggers inventory deduction if deduct_inventory=true
- preparing ΓåÆ ready: Notifies for serving
- ready ΓåÆ served ΓåÆ completed: Updates billing/payment

Query param `deduct_inventory` can be set to false to skip deduction (for re-printing etc.)
Update individual item status in order

post.description:
Start preparing an order in kitchen.
This triggers:
1. Order status changed to 'preparing'
2. Inventory deduction based on recipes
3. Kitchen timer starts

Mark order as ready in kitchen.
This triggers:
1. Order status changed to 'ready'
2. Notification sent to waiters

Complete order serving.
This triggers:
1. Order status changed to 'completed'
2. Payment status updated
3. Order completion time recorded

get.description:
Get all active orders for kitchen display.
Returns orders in: placed, preparing, ready states

post.description:
Complete workflow for processing an order.
Coordinates between Orders, Kitchen, Inventory, and Billing.

Expected data:
{
    "orderId": "...",
    "action": "start_preparing|mark_ready|serve|complete|cancel",
    "items": [...] // Only needed for start_preparing
}

Migration endpoint to fix the duplicate key error on orders.
Drops the problematic 'id' index and removes 'id' field from all documents.

Get staff performance analytics derived from actual orders and attendance

post.description:
Handle guest arrival - transitions from 'reserved' or 'walk-in-blocked' to 'occupied'
Sets up for waiter assignment and order taking

Handle walk-in booking - blocks table for 15 minutes waiting for guest arrival
If guest doesn't arrive in 15 minutes, automatically return to available

Expected data:
{
    "guestCount": 4,
    "customerName": "John Doe"
}

Handle waiter assignment - waiter is ready to take order
This is called after waiter is assigned to the table

Expected data:
{
    "waiterId": "waiter_id",
    "waiterName": "John"
}

Handle order creation - links order to table
Called when waiter creates an order for the table

Expected data:
{
    "orderId": "order_id",
    "orderNumber": "#ORD-1001"
}

Handle order acceptance - order is confirmed and sent to kitchen
Updates table status to 'order_accepted'

Expected data:
{
    "orderId": "order_id",
    "kitchenStatus": "accepted"
}

Handle order preparation - chef has taken the order and started preparing
Updates table status to 'order_preparing' (or 'eating')

Expected data:
{
    "orderId": "order_id",
    "chefId": "chef_id",
    "estimatedTimeMinutes": 20
}

Handle payment completion - payment is done, start cleaning process
Updates table status to 'checked_out', then schedules transition to 'cleaning'
After 5 minutes, table returns to 'available' or original 'reserved' status

Expected data:
{
    "billId": "bill_id",
    "paymentId": "payment_id",
    "amount": 500.00,
    "paymentMethod": "cash",
    "originalStatus": "available"  # What status to return to after cleaning
}

get.description: Return invoices for a specific customer (by userId / email).

post.description:
Activate a reservation when the customer arrives / time slot starts.
- Sets reservation status to Active
- Changes table status to occupied
- Auto-assigns the waiter with the fewest currently-assigned tables

get.description:
Returns publicly visible restaurant stats used on the home page:
- totalDishes      : available menu items
- happyCustomers   : unique customer count (from orders)
- tablesAvailable  : tables currently available
- ordersToday      : orders placed today
